stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core fields of transformers and text-generation, indicating relevance in the LLM space.
The inclusion of 'llama' and 'Meta' confirms the focus on a major, established player in the generative AI industry.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate its relevance within the transformers and safetensors ecosystem.

stories.title: Nvidia bets $150B on Taiwan as Trump's plan to make US an AI hub backfires
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Nvidia is committing a massive $150 billion annual investment to Taiwan.
The stated goal of this investment is to establish Taiwan as a global AI 'epicenter'.
The move is framed against the backdrop of political shifts, specifically referencing the backfiring of a Trump-era plan to centralize AI in the US.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating active development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI functionality for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being deployed in partnership with ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Bad cholesterol slashed 62% by single dose of gene-editing drug in small trial
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: A gene-editing drug demonstrated a significant reduction (62%) in bad cholesterol in a small trial.
The findings are based on interim Phase I trial data involving only 35 participants.
This represents a potential major medical breakthrough in cardiovascular health treatment.

stories.title: CubePart: An Open-Vocabulary Part-Controllable 3D Generator
stories.author: Tinghui Zhou
bullet_points: CubePart is a new generative framework for creating 3D meshes with open-vocabulary, part-controllable capabilities.
It addresses a key limitation in current 3D generation models by explicitly exposing part structure as a control signal.
This advancement is highly relevant for applications like gaming and simulation, where semantic part decomposition is crucial for animation and physics.

stories.title: Bias Leaves a Gradient Trail: Label-Free Bias Identification via Gradient Probes on Concept Decompositions
stories.author: Jae Hee Lee
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, label-free method to identify spurious correlations (bias) in pre-trained vision models.
The technique uses 'gradient probes' on concept decompositions, allowing analysis without needing curated bias datasets or specific attribute labels.
This post-hoc approach is highly relevant for deployed models where retraining or extensive labeling is impractical.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture and utilizes standard ML formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model trending on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests an ongoing evaluation or demonstration of large language model performance.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Claude’s new model is more ‘honest’ when it messes up
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Anthropic is launching Claude Opus 4.8, emphasizing a focus on model 'honesty'.
The new training emphasizes preventing models from making unsubstantiated claims.
The article acknowledges that jumping to conclusions remains a general challenge in AI models.

stories.title: A $2,000 AI-generated film will make its debut at Tribeca
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: An AI-generated film titled 'Dreams of Violets' will premiere at the Tribeca Festival.
The 75-minute film uses AI to create all its people and imagery, with a reported production cost of $2,000.
The film is a fictional dramatization concerning the Iranian government's mass killing of protestors.

stories.title: Trump loses more control over AI regulation as Illinois passes landmark law
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Illinois has passed a landmark law concerning AI regulation.
The legislation involves safety testing protocols for major AI developers like Anthropic and OpenAI.
The story analyzes the implications of this state-level regulation despite broader political shifts regarding AI control.

stories.title: Websites have a new way to spy on visitors: Analyzing their SSD activity
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Websites can now monitor visitor activity by analyzing Solid State Drive (SSD) usage.
This surveillance capability can be implemented using straightforward JavaScript within the browser.
The development represents a new, subtle vector for web tracking and privacy concerns.

stories.title: Mystery GPS jammer in Iran becomes test for NASA satellites’ capabilities
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: NASA satellites are being utilized to detect and locate sources of GPS interference.
The incident involves a mystery GPS jammer operating in Iran.
The story highlights the dual-use capabilities of NASA science satellites for surveillance/interference detection.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the 'sim-to-real' gap in dexterous manipulation, a major bottleneck in robotics.
Proposes a novel method, 'Center-of-Pressure,' to overcome the limitation of simplifying rich tactile data.
Focuses on improving the use of information-dense modalities like touch in simulation-based reinforcement learning.

stories.title: Calibrating Conservatism for Scalable Oversight
stories.author: Mohsen Bayati
bullet_points: Addresses the fundamental control problem of maintaining human oversight over highly capable, autonomous AI agents.
Introduces 'Calibrated Collective Oversight' (CCO) as a new framework for scalable oversight.
CCO aggregates diverse auxiliary scoring functions to provide oversight with statistical guarantees, addressing limitations of current heuristic methods.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: Introduces OmniVerifier-M1, a multimodal meta-verifier designed to improve the reliability of multimodal large language models (LLMs).
The research focuses on leveraging verifier-generated rationales, moving beyond simple decision signals for more fine-grained verification.
Investigates methods to effectively incorporate meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: Introduces CaMBRAIN, a new method for real-time, continuous EEG inference.
Addresses limitations of existing EEG models, specifically the quadratic scaling of attention mechanisms with long sequences.
Proposes using Causal State Space Models to overcome the fixed-length input constraints of traditional sliding-window processing.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond traditional methods that rely on trusted, privileged information (PI) like reference answers.
Proposes using an experience-derived 'skill bank' as PI, acknowledging the risk of irrelevant or misleading skills.

stories.title: Do Agents Need Semantic Metadata? A Comparative Study in Agentic Data Retrieval
stories.author: Natasha Noy
bullet_points: The paper investigates the necessity of traditional semantic metadata (like schema.org) in the context of autonomous AI agents.
It addresses the shift in data retrieval capabilities due to the rise of Large Language Models (LLMs) navigating unstructured web data.
The core question explores whether LLMs can render established FAIR principles and metadata standards obsolete for agentic workflows.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a novel memory framework for LLM agents that moves beyond static memory repositories.
Models memory as a heterogeneous graph whose topology is progressively refined based on dynamic environmental feedback.
Addresses the brittleness of current memory-augmented LLMs in complex, dynamic agentic environments.

stories.title: SwarmHarness: Skill-Based Task Routing via Decentralized Incentive-Aligned AI Agent Networks
stories.author: Edwin Jose
bullet_points: SwarmHarness proposes a decentralized protocol to utilize vast amounts of unused compute resources (GPUs, edge devices).
It addresses limitations of existing systems, such as reliance on central coordinators or heavy blockchain infrastructure.
The core innovation is an incentive-aligned AI agent network designed to allow owners to share compute safely and profitably.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) of LLMs.
Addresses a gap in current PEFT evaluations by focusing on capability retention alongside downstream accuracy.
Frames the evaluation around the stability-plasticity dilemma: balancing task adaptation with resistance to catastrophic forgetting.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the 'sim-to-real' gap in robotic manipulation, specifically concerning contact-rich tasks.
Proposes a novel approach, 'Center-of-Pressure,' to overcome the limitations of simplifying rich tactile data in simulations.
Aims to enable more complex, dexterous manipulation by effectively bridging the gap between simulation and the real world.

stories.title: Affective Music Recommendation: A Rollout-Based World Model for Offline Preference Optimization
stories.author: Laurent Charlin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of recommending music based on a listener's affective state when online emotional experimentation is ethically constrained.
Introduces AMRS, an Affective Music Recommendation System deployed on health-and-wellness platforms.
Utilizes a Rollout-Based World Model to optimize offline preferences for functional music applications (e.g., sleep aids, clinical interventions).

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: Introduces OmniVerifier-M1, a multimodal meta-verifier designed to improve the reliability of multimodal large language models.
The research focuses on leveraging verifier-generated rationales (not just binary decisions) to enhance verification processes.
It explores methods for effectively incorporating meta-verification feedback into the training of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Ω-QVLA: Robust Quantization for Vision-Language-Action Models via Composite Rotation and Per-step Scaling
stories.author: Peng Lu
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which are crucial for unifying perception, reasoning, and control.
Proposes a novel quantization technique, Ω-QVLA, to enable on-device deployment of these large models.
Overcomes limitations in prior work by addressing the instability of quantizing diffusion-based action heads, which are often kept at full precision.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: Introduces CaMBRAIN, a new approach for real-time, continuous EEG inference.
Addresses limitations of existing EEG models, specifically the quadratic scaling issue of attention mechanisms with long sequences.
Overcomes the constraint of fixed-length inputs by moving away from traditional sliding-window processing.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a new memory framework for LLM agents that treats memory as a continuously evolving heterogeneous graph.
Addresses the limitation of current memory-augmented LLMs, which use static repositories that are brittle in dynamic environments.
The framework progressively refines the memory topology based on continuous feedback and task variation.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) in LLMs.
Addresses a gap in current PEFT evaluations by focusing on the trade-off between task adaptation and capability retention (stability-plasticity).
Jointly measures both downstream performance and the model's ability to retain general pretrained knowledge.

stories.title: VLMs May Not Globally Enhance Human Alignment over LLMs During Natural Reading
stories.author: Zitong Lu
bullet_points: The study investigates whether Vision-Language Models (VLMs) inherently lead to more human-like text representations compared to Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research isolates the effect of multimodal training history by comparing tightly matched LLM and VLM pairs in a strictly text-only evaluation setting.
The findings suggest that VLM training may not globally enhance human alignment over LLMs when analyzing natural reading comprehension.

stories.title: Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search
stories.author: Yilun Du
bullet_points: Proposes a novel method, 'Bidirectional Evolutionary Search,' to enhance self-improving language models.
Addresses limitations of existing search methods (like best-of-N and tree search) which are restricted by sparse verification signals and autoregressive expansion.
Focuses on improving both post-training sample generation and inference capabilities of agentic systems.

stories.title: Personal Visual Memory from Explicit and Implicit Evidence
stories.author: Yuheng Li
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI memory systems, which are predominantly text-centric, despite the growing need for personalized AI agents.
Highlights that images often contain crucial personal information (explicit and implicit) that is frequently lost or reduced to generic captions in existing models.
Proposes a method to leverage both explicit and implicit visual evidence for building more robust, personalized long-term memory in AI.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for reliable, fine-grained verification in multimodal large language models (LLMs).
It introduces 'multimodal meta-verification,' which uses verifier-generated rationales instead of simple decision signals.
The work explores methods to effectively incorporate meta-verification feedback into the training of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond relying on trusted, pre-defined privileged information (PI) for supervision.
Proposes using an experience-derived, reusable, but potentially noisy 'skill bank' as the source of PI.

stories.title: Can Large Language Models Handle Discourse Particles? A Case Study of Colloquial Malay
stories.author: Xi Chen
bullet_points: The research investigates the capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to process discourse particles, which are vital for human-like expression of emotion and intention.
A key gap identified is the lack of comprehensive LLM studies on discourse particles, particularly in low-resource or non-English languages.
The study specifically focuses on Colloquial Malay, addressing the underrepresentation of Southeast Asian languages in current LLM research.

stories.title: The Abstraction Gap in Vision-Language Causal Reasoning
stories.author: Mohammad Rashedul Hasan
bullet_points: Addresses a critical limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): current evaluations cannot separate linguistic fluency from true causal reasoning.
Proposes a novel 'dual-probe methodology' to isolate and measure these two distinct properties (linguistic plausibility vs. causal faithfulness).
Introduces the 'Abstraction Gap (AG) metric' to quantify the performance difference across various VLMs on causal reasoning tasks.

stories.title: AI Agents Are Great at 80% of Our Code. The Other 20% Is Why We Still Need Seniors.
stories.author: mickyarun
bullet_points: AI agents demonstrated high proficiency (80%) in handling routine coding tasks on a payment platform.
The study highlights that AI agents struggle with the remaining 20% of complex or edge-case code.
The necessity of senior developers remains crucial for handling the difficult, non-trivial aspects of software development.
